title: Moog Technology Keeps Artemis II Astronauts Safe During Historic Lunar Mission
summary: Moog Inc. provided critical technology to ensure the safety of astronauts during NASA's Artemis II lunar mission. The company's precision motion and fluid control systems played a key role in the historic mission.

title: Orion splashes down to successfully end Artemis 2 mission
summary: The Artemis 2 mission, the first human mission beyond Earth orbit in over 50 years, successfully concluded with the Orion spacecraft splashing down in the Pacific Ocean on April 10. This marked a significant milestone for NASA's lunar exploration program.

title: HawkEye 360 files to go public
summary: HawkEye 360, a space-based radio-frequency signals intelligence company based in Herndon, Virginia, has filed to go public. The company provides services to government agencies.

title: Spacety raises $190 million to scale satellite manufacturing, plans IPO
summary: Spacety, a Chinese satellite manufacturer, raised $190 million in equity financing to expand its vertically integrated satellite production and data services. The company also announced plans for an initial public offering (IPO).

title: GSOA and Novaspace Launch the Space Industry Forum (SIF) 2026 in Singapore
summary: GSOA and Novaspace are launching the Space Industry Forum (SIF) 2026, a new flagship event scheduled for May 19, 2026, at The Fullerton Hotel in Singapore.

title: Commerce Department budget proposal revives concerns about TraCSS
summary: A budget proposal for the Department of Commerce has raised concerns about the future of the civil space traffic management system (TraCSS) under development. This could impact the progress and implementation of the system.

title: China’s Chang’e-7 arrives at spaceport for lunar south pole exploration mission
summary: China's Chang'e-7 lunar spacecraft has arrived at Wenchang spaceport for launch preparations. The mission, targeting the lunar south pole, is scheduled to launch in the second half of 2026.

title: NASA prepares for Artemis 2 return
summary: NASA is preparing for the Artemis 2 mission's return, with the spacecraft scheduled to reenter on April 10 before splashing down off the California coast.

title: PLD Space secures $35 million European Investment Bank loan for MIURA 5 launcher
summary: PLD Space secured a $35 million loan from the European Investment Bank to fund the final development of its MIURA 5 lightweight rocket. The MIURA 5 is designed to deploy payloads of up to 1 ton to low Earth orbit.

title: Heather Pringle on the upcoming Space Symposium and future international collaboration in space
summary: Heather Pringle, CEO of a major space organization, discusses upcoming Space Symposium and future international collaboration in space on the Space Minds podcast. The interview highlights her insights as a retired US Air Force Major General.

title: Splashdown! Artemis 2 astronauts return to Earth after historic NASA mission to the moon
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts successfully returned to Earth on April 10, completing a historic mission around the moon. The mission set new spaceflight records and paved the way for future lunar exploration.

title: NEAF 2026 returns to New York this weekend with all-star astronaut lineup and 'world's largest' space expo
summary: NEAF 2026, the world's largest space expo, returns to New York with an all-star astronaut lineup. The event will attract thousands of enthusiasts, professionals, and skywatchers.

title: The Artemis 2 astronauts saw a rare solar eclipse from beyond the moon. Here's what it looked like
summary: The Artemis 2 astronauts witnessed a rare solar eclipse from beyond the moon, captured in a NASA timelapse showing the moon crossing the sun and revealing the solar corona.

title: Why I'm hunting for Comet Pan-STARRS right now — before it's too late
summary: A rare long-period comet, C/2025 R3 (Pan-STARRS), will be visible before sunrise in April after 170,000 years. Stargazers are encouraged to observe it with key dates and viewing tips provided.

title: 'They’re not shipping cows up to Mars': 'For All Mankind' creators talk scientific accuracy and colonial inspirations behind season 5 (interview)
summary: The creators of the TV show 'For All Mankind' discuss the scientific accuracy and colonial inspirations behind season 5. The interview highlights the show's dramatic arc and real-world parallels.

title: NASA took this camera gear to space aboard Artemis 2, and you can own it, too!
summary: NASA is auctioning off camera gear used aboard the Artemis 2 mission, the first crewed lunar mission in over 50 years. The cameras, which captured stunning space imagery, are now available for public purchase.

title: Artemis 2 spies a crescent Earth above the moon's horizon | Space photo of the day for April 10, 2026
summary: A new photo from the Artemis 2 mission captures Earth as a crescent above the moon's horizon. This image highlights the mission's progress and its focus on lunar exploration.

title: Ripples in spacetime may have revealed 1st evidence of tiny black holes born in the Big Bang
summary: Gravitational waves may have revealed the first evidence of tiny primordial black holes formed during the Big Bang, which could explain dark matter. This discovery, if confirmed, would revolutionize our understanding of the universe's composition.

title: Watch NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts return to Earth live online today (April 10)
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts are returning to Earth today after a 10-day mission around the moon. Live coverage of their splashdown is available online.

title: Where will NASA's Artemis 2 moon astronauts make their splashdown today?
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts are scheduled to splash down in the Pacific Ocean today (April 10). The chosen landing site was selected based on specific mission requirements and safety considerations.

title: NASA Welcomes Record-Setting Artemis II Moonfarers Back to Earth
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission, carrying astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and CSA's Jeremy Hansen, successfully splashed down off the coast of Earth after a historic Moon flyby. This marks the first crewed lunar mission in over 50 years.

title: New Perspective of Home
summary: Artemis II's lunar flyby on April 6, 2026, will capture a unique perspective of the Moon and Earth aligned in the same frame, showcasing detailed lunar surface features and Earth's soft illumination in the background.

title: Earthset From the Lunar Far Side
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission crew captured stunning images of Earth from the far side of the Moon. This marks a significant milestone in lunar exploration and human spaceflight.

title: Indoor Testing Facilities available at the NASA Unmanned Autonomy Research Complex (NUARC)
summary: NASA's Unmanned Autonomy Research Complex (NUARC) offers indoor testing facilities like the WindShaper fan array for dynamic low-speed and hovering flight research, including arbitrary wind gradients and gusts via a Python API. A companion WindProbe is also available for flow surveys using the lab's OptiTrack motion capture system.

title: I Am Artemis: Dan Florez
summary: NASA's Artemis I mission launched on November 16, 2022, at 1:47 a.m. EST, with Dan Florez, test director for the Exploration Ground Systems Program, observing from Kennedy Space Center's Launch Control Center.

title: Indoor Testing Facilities available at the NASA Unmanned Autonomy Research Complex (NUARC)
summary: NASA's Unmanned Autonomy Research Complex (NUARC) offers indoor testing facilities with a WindShaper fan array for dynamic low-speed and hovering flight research, including arbitrary wind gradient generation via Python API. A companion WindProbe is also available for flow surveys using the lab’s OptiTrack motion capture system.

title: Starstruck
summary: The Artemis II crew captured a photo of the Milky Way galaxy on April 7, 2026, showcasing its spiral structure with two dominant arms extending from a central bar of stars.

title: Watching the Artemis II Mission Unfold at JPL’s Space Flight Operations Facility
summary: NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory staff will monitor the Artemis II mission from the Space Flight Operations Facility in Southern California after its planned launch on April 1, 2026. The Deep Space Network (DSN), operated from this facility, includes complexes in Goldstone, Madrid, and Canberra.

title: The Deep Space Network Acquires Artemis II Signal
summary: NASA's Deep Space Network successfully acquired a signal from the Artemis II mission to the Moon, scheduled for April 1, 2026. The signal was received by antennas at the Madrid Deep Space Communications Complex.

title: The Deep Space Network Acquires Artemis II Signal
summary: NASA's Deep Space Network successfully acquired the radio signal from the Artemis II crewed lunar mission shortly after its launch on April 1, 2026. The milestone marks a key step in tracking the spacecraft's journey to the Moon.

title: Artemis II returns to Earth after historic 10-day mission around the Moon
summary: Artemis II completed a historic 10-day mission around the Moon, traveling over 800,000 km before returning to Earth. This marks a significant milestone in NASA's lunar exploration program.

title: Artemis II closing in on Earth return
summary: Artemis II is nearing its return to Earth after a historic 10-day mission that took humans farther from Earth than any previous crewed mission. The mission marks a significant milestone in NASA's Artemis program.

title: China debuts new launchers, tests orbital servicing and outlines future deep-space missions
summary: China has introduced new commercial launchers from CAS Space and Space Pioneer, while also testing orbital servicing capabilities and outlining future deep-space missions.

title: SpaceX continues refinements at Starbase Pad 2 ahead of Booster 19 return
summary: SpaceX is making final preparations at Starbase Pad 2 for the return of Booster 19. The refinements are part of ongoing operations at the company's South Texas launch site.

title: Artemis II breaks record, conducts lunar flyby
summary: Artemis II achieved a record-breaking lunar flyby with the Orion spacecraft Integrity, carrying astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen. This mission marks a significant milestone in NASA's Artemis program.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to loft Cygnus to ISS, Minotaur IV to launch from California
summary: A Falcon 9 rocket is set to launch a Cygnus spacecraft to the International Space Station (ISS), while a Minotaur IV rocket is scheduled to launch from California. These launches occur as NASA's Artemis II mission progresses.

title: Orion Spacecraft Races Toward Historic Lunar Flyby in Artemis II Mission
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission is in its mid-flight phase as the Orion spacecraft approaches a historic lunar flyby. This marks a significant milestone for the mission, which aims to advance lunar exploration.

title: Artemis II Orion performs TLI Burn — Now headed to the Moon
summary: NASA's Orion spacecraft successfully performed the Trans-Lunar Injection (TLI) burn, marking a historic milestone as it heads to the Moon. This achievement is part of the Artemis II mission, the first crewed lunar mission in over 50 years.

title: Artemis II returns humanity to the Moon with launch on Wednesday evening
summary: The Artemis II mission aims to return humanity to the Moon with a launch scheduled for Wednesday evening. This marks a significant milestone in space exploration following the Apollo 17 mission in 1972.

title: Launch Preview: SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz launches comprise busy launch manifest
summary: Nine launches are scheduled this week, featuring rockets like SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz lifting off from Florida, California, and Russia.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026

title: Pointing in the (b)right direction
summary: The article discusses advancements in spacecraft pointing technology, likely focusing on improved precision or efficiency in directional control. It highlights a specific innovation or achievement in this area.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team demonstrated light-based propulsion using graphene aerogels during ESA's parabolic flight campaign in May 2025. The experiment showed that lasers could propel these ultralight materials in microgravity, offering potential for future satellite and solar sail applications.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: Lasers and graphene could revolutionize space propulsion by steering solar sails and adjusting satellite positions. An experiment simulating zero gravity demonstrated the potential of this innovative material.

title: A silicon chip that can do it all
summary: A new silicon chip has been developed that integrates multiple functions, potentially revolutionizing space industry applications. This breakthrough could enhance satellite and spacecraft capabilities.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long anomaly. The mission team confirmed the spacecraft is operational and communicating again.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to discuss the implementation of decisions from the CM25 ministerial meeting, aiming to inform industry about upcoming activities and opportunities within ESA programs.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato mission, designed to discover Earth-like exoplanets, is undergoing space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator chamber. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, is set for a January 2027 launch on an Ariane 6 rocket.

title: Albedo Ratchets Up the Power for its Second VLEO Flight
summary: Albedo unveiled a new VLEO-capable satellite bus, targeting a second orbital mission in 2027. This development aims to enhance the company's capabilities in Very Low Earth Orbit (VLEO) operations.

title: Moog’s “Tip to Tail” Contributions to the Artemis II Flight
summary: Moog has supplied over 100 components for the Artemis II mission, covering hardware from the rocket to the Orion spacecraft. The company's contributions highlight its role in supporting NASA's lunar program.

title: Artemis II Gives Airbus Hope For European Spaceflight
summary: The Artemis II mission features the European Service Module (ESM), built by Airbus Defence and Space, which strengthens Europe's role in spaceflight. This collaboration between Airbus and ESA highlights Europe's growing involvement in NASA's lunar program.

title: Portal Closes $50M Series A
summary: Portal has secured a $50 million Series A funding round led by Geodesic Capital and Mach33, with additional investments from Booz Allen Ventures, Ark Invest, AlleyCorp, and FUSE.

title: The State of Launch 2026
summary: Rocket Lab's VP of launch discusses the impact of deadlines and capability gates in the space industry, highlighting how constraints can influence competition and outcomes in launch services.

title: Vantor Unveils New Sat Classes: Vantage and Pulse
summary: Vantor introduced two new satellite classes, Vantage and Pulse, aimed at enhancing imagery resolution and revisit rate. This marks a significant step in the company's technological advancements in satellite imaging.

title: The State of ISAM 2026
summary: The article discusses the growing complexity of In-Space Servicing, Assembly, and Manufacturing (ISAM) by 2026, noting that it can no longer be treated as a single concept. It highlights the expanding scope and challenges of ISAM as a field.

title: Czech Radiation Chips on Artemis II Bring ISS Experience
summary: Czech-developed radiation chips will be part of the Artemis II mission, leveraging ISS experience to monitor solar flares during solar maximum. HERA will act as an early warning system for potential solar flare events.

title: Advocates Ready For NASA Science Funding Fight, Part II
summary: Advocates and lawmakers are preparing to oppose the Trump administration's proposed $18.8 billion NASA budget, which includes significant cuts to science and STEM missions. This marks a recurring conflict over NASA's funding priorities.

title: Starfish Closes $100M+ Series B
summary: Starfish Space has secured over $100 million in a Series B funding round led by Point72 Ventures, with additional participation from Activate Capital and Shield Capital.

title: Emirates Connectivity Exec Explains the Airline’s Move to Starlink
summary: Emirates announced its adoption of Starlink for in-flight Wi-Fi in November, starting deployment across its entire fleet. The airline's connectivity executive explained the strategic move to enhance passenger connectivity.

title: Amazon CEO Targets Mid-2026 Launch for Amazon Leo Service
summary: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy announced in a shareholder letter that Amazon's satellite constellation service, Amazon Leo, is planned to launch by mid-2026. No specific launch date was provided.

title: BryceTech Report Shows SpaceX Accounted for 50% of Launches in 2025
summary: A BryceTech report reveals that SpaceX accounted for 50% of all launches in 2025. The report highlights a shift in the space industry toward small satellites.

title: EnduroSat and Shield Space Strike European Partnership for Defense Missions
summary: EnduroSat and Shield Space have formed a strategic partnership to jointly operate space defense missions in Europe. The collaboration aims to leverage their combined technologies for on-orbit operations.

title: Network Integration Remains a Challenge in Pushing Data to the Tactical Edge
summary: Commercial space leaders are working to push data from centralized nodes to the tactical edge for faster decision-making in conflict scenarios. Network integration remains a key challenge in this effort.

title: Vantor Introduces New Satellite Classes for High Resolution Imaging, Frequent Revisits
summary: Vantor unveiled two new classes of electro-optical imaging satellites designed for high-resolution imagery and frequent revisits. The innovation aims to enhance commercial satellite operations and spatial intelligence services.

title: Space Force Awards 14 Companies Space Domain Awareness Contracts Under “Andromeda”
summary: The U.S. Space Force awarded 14 companies over $1.8 billion in firm fixed price contracts under the 'Andromeda' program for Geosynchronous Space Domain Awareness. Space Systems Command (SSC) announced the contracts aimed at enhancing space domain awareness capabilities.

title: NSG Names Haithem Alfaraj as its Second CEO
summary: Neo Space Group (NSG), a Saudi space company, has appointed Haithem Mohammed Mojil Alfaraj as its new CEO, replacing Martijn Blanken who led the company since its inception.

title: In-Space Maneuverability Startup Portal Raises Series A as it Shifts into Production Mode
summary: Portal Space Systems raised $50 million in a Series A funding round to advance its maneuverable spacecraft technology and expand manufacturing. The startup is transitioning into production mode with a growing team.

title: Live in the Booth: Sky Perfect JSAT President & CEO Eiichi Yonekura
summary: Sky Perfect JSAT, a major global satellite operator, is focusing on a multi-faceted strategy to expand its business in the coming years. The company's President & CEO, Eiichi Yonekura, discussed these plans in a recent interview.
